title: g-tensor Optimization in Ge/SiGe Quantum Dots
authors: Aram Shojaei, Edmondo Valvo, Maximilian Rimbach-Russ, Eliska Greplova, Ana Silva
abstract: Planar germanium heterostructures hosting hole-spin qubits are among the leading platforms for scalable semiconductor-based quantum computing. Yet, device performance is hindered by significant quantum dot variability, which leads to uncertainty in qubit energy levels and random orientations of the spin quantization axis. Tailored control of the g-tensor offers a strategy to overcome these limitations and achieve more reliable qubit operations. Here, we introduce a flexible optimization framework for engineering g-tensor properties. As a benchmark, we numerically obtain the optimal reshaping of the out-of-plane potential in a SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum well to suppress the in-plane g-tensor components and realize the recently proposed gapless single-spin qubit encoding. This reshaping is achieved through heterostructure engineering, specifically by adjusting the silicon concentration within the quantum well, though the framework remains readily adaptable to alternative design objectives. Our results provide practical design principles for improving the tunability of the spin response, paving the way towards large-scale germanium-based quantum computers.
summary: This paper presents a numerical framework to optimize the g-tensor in germanium-based quantum dots by engineering the heterostructure potential. By adjusting silicon concentration, the researchers show it is possible to suppress unwanted g-tensor components, which helps mitigate device variability and enables more reliable qubit operations for scalable quantum computing.
main_problem: Device performance in planar germanium hole-spin qubit platforms is limited by quantum dot variability, which causes uncertain energy levels and random spin quantization axis orientations.
main_result: The authors developed a flexible optimization framework that demonstrates how reshaping the out-of-plane potential via silicon concentration adjustment can suppress in-plane g-tensor components to enable gapless single-spin qubit encoding.
method: A numerical optimization framework designed for engineering g-tensor properties through heterostructure engineering.
model_or_system: A SiGe-Ge-SiGe quantum well heterostructure hosting hole-spin qubits.
key_observables: g-tensor components (specifically in-plane vs. out-of-plane) and the spin quantization axis orientation.
important_parameters: Silicon concentration within the quantum well and the resulting out-of-plane potential profile.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of variability in Ge/SiGe quantum dots, presents a new optimization framework for g-tensor engineering, uses the suppression of in-plane g-tensor components as a benchmark case, and concludes with the implications for scalable quantum computing.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
